title: Heatwave Alert: IMD Predicts Intense Heat Conditions From April 20-25; Advisory Issued For Key States | Forecast - The Daily Jagran
full_text:
Severe heat conditions are expected for a four to five-day period over most of northern India, including much of the Northern Plains, according to the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD). Maximum daytime high temperatures should be above the norm and are expected to exceed 45 degrees Celsius in many areas of North India.
The IMD is advising residents to:

Heat Wave Advisory
The IMD is advising of expected heat wave conditions across western, central, and eastern India, beginning April 20 through April 25.
Affected regions include:
IMD is also forecasting extremely warm, humid weather in the Gangetic West Bengal region from April 20 through April 26.
In addition, IMD has predicted heat wave conditions will develop in Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, and Karaikal from April 20 through April 22; in Kerala, Mahe, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, and Yanam from April 20 through April 24; and in Coastal Gujarat from April 24 through April 25.

Warm night conditions are also likely on April 21 in Haryana, Chandigarh, western Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, and Vidarbha. Odisha may experience heatwave conditions between April 20 and 22.
The weather agency further stated that moderate to strong winds are expected across parts of southern, central, eastern, and northwest India. Isolated hailstorms are also possible in central Maharashtra, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, and Chhattisgarh over the next 24 hours.
